title: epipactis albensis subsp. moravica
intro: Epipactis albensis subsp. moravica is a subspecies of the Epipactis albensis orchid, a type of terrestrial orchid native to Europe. It is used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues, and it is also used to treat skin conditions and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
